scenario: Nimbus Bank's modeling team builds a Camunda 8.8 DMN diagram in Web Modeler to review 3000 loan applications per day. After placing a decision element on the canvas, the lead modeler wants to start editing the rules in the decision table view as quickly as possible without retracing through the palette.
question: Which modeler action lets the team start editing the rules of a decision element in the decision table view?

options.text: Morph the decision into a decision table and then click the overlay on the upper-left corner of the decision to open the decision table view
Open the right-side properties panel of the decision and switch a View attribute from DRD to decision table to enter table editing mode
Drag the decision off the palette a second time so the modeler swaps it for a decision table element and lands directly in the table view
Right-click the decision on the canvas and choose an Open in decision table command from the context menu to jump into the table view directly

a.text: Correct. The DMN docs describe the workflow as creating a decision, morphing it into a decision table, and then starting to edit the table by clicking the overlay on the upper-left corner of the decision.

b.text: Incorrect. The properties panel edits the name or ID of the DMN diagram; the docs do not describe a View attribute that switches the editor between DRD view and decision table view.

c.text: Incorrect. The DMN docs do not describe a drag-twice gesture for swapping a decision; the element type is changed using the Change element menu icon on the context menu.

d.text: Incorrect. The DMN docs entry point to decision table editing is the overlay on the upper-left corner of the morphed decision, not an Open in decision table context-menu command.

explanation: After creating a decision and morphing it into a decision table, the DMN docs say you start editing the table by clicking the overlay on the upper-left corner of the decision. The Overview on the left side then lets you jump between decision tables and literal expressions in the DRD.
